appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.languageHint: Applies to UI labels, server messages, and the assistant's replies.

defaultModel.label: Default model
defaultModel.title: Default model
defaultModel.description: This is the model used when you start a new conversation without choosing another. You can switch at any time inside chat.
defaultModel.noModelsRegistered: No models registered yet. Go to <bold>Models</bold> to add a cloud model (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) or install one locally.
defaultModel.selectAbove: Select a model above to set it as default.

credentials.sectionDescription: API keys and tokens stored at ~/.nexus/secrets.toml (file mode 0600). Use them in skills as $NAME placeholders — the server substitutes the value at the tool boundary, the LLM never sees the raw key. Environment variables of the same name take precedence and are not listed here.
credentials.valueHint: Stored locally; never sent to the LLM.
credentials.skillHint: Tag the credential as belonging to a specific skill, for your own reference.
credentials.editModalMessage: Enter the new value. The current value is replaced; nothing else changes.
credentials.deleteModalMessage: This removes the value from ~/.nexus/secrets.toml. Skills that depend on it will be re-prompted on next use.

advanced.agentBehaviorDescription: Generation fine-tuning. The defaults work well in most cases.
advanced.maxStepsHint: Cap on tool-loop iterations before the agent stops. High values allow complex tasks; low values guard against loops.
advanced.temperatureHint: 0 = deterministic (recommended for tool-calling). Higher values raise creativity at the cost of unstable output.
advanced.frequencyPenaltyHint: Lowers the chance of already-used tokens reappearing. Useful against local models that get stuck in loops.
advanced.presencePenaltyHint: Nudges the model toward new topics. Default 0 (no effect).
advanced.antiRepeatHint: Aborts the stream if the output keeps repeating the same pattern for N characters. 0 disables.
advanced.maxOutputHint: Cap on tokens generated per LLM call. 0 = unlimited (provider default); Anthropic falls back to 4096 since its API requires the field. Per-model overrides win when set.
advanced.autoApprovalDescription: When a dangerous tool asks for confirmation before running, the agent normally opens a dialog. With auto-approval enabled, yes/no questions are answered automatically.
advanced.autoApproveHint: Doesn't affect open-ended text or multiple-choice questions — you'll still need to answer those.
advanced.soundsDescription: Subtle tones for final response, notifications, popups and agent steps. Sounds are synthesized locally — no audio is downloaded.
advanced.silentModeHint: When on, all sound effects are suppressed.
